method_atom: A weighted, signed counting invariant (a character-valued generating series that traces over a state space with statistics-dependent signs so that only a robust subset — configurations meeting a protection constraint and not combinable into cancelling groupings — contributes) built as a formal series in auxiliary variables. This invariant is shown to be preserved under a nontrivial equivalence between two differently parameterized descriptions of the same system by invoking proven transformation identities for a family of elliptic / q-series hypergeometric integrals, so that matching the two integral forms certifies the equivalence.
object_atom: N=1 superconformal supersymmetric gauge theories — specifically SQCD with SU(N_c), Sp(N), and SO(N) gauge groups and N_f flavours, plus Kutasov-Schwimmer duals in the large N_c, N_f limit — whose spectrum of BPS-protected operators (scalar and vector multiplets) is enumerated, with the equivalence being Seiberg duality relating electric and magnetic descriptions.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is a signed generating-function invariant that counts protection-constrained configurations and is proven invariant across two descriptions via special-function integral identities; the domain-specific object is the supersymmetric gauge theories and their dual pairs, and method and object here are cleanly separable.
